title: Incident Channel Mix Trending
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-180d@d
| eval ch=lower(coalesce(contact_type, channel, u_channel, "unknown"))
| eval _time=coalesce(opened_at, _time)
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count by _time, ch
| eventstats sum(count) as week_total by _time
| eval share_pct=round(100*count/nullif(week_total,0),1)
| sort _time ch
description: Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service ROI; trending aligns workforce management with ITIL service desk strategy and digital adoption goals.
value: Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service ROI; trending aligns workforce management with ITIL service desk strategy and digital adoption goals.
implementation: (1) Normalize `ch` with a lookup for legacy values; (2) Exclude automated monitoring tickets via `caller_id` or `category` filters; (3) Review rising phone share weekly with service owners when portal adoption programs are active.
visualization: Stacked area chart (channel share % over weeks), Line chart (portal % trend), Table (raw counts by channel).
knownFalsePositives: Shifts in how people reach us after portal, chat, and phone rollouts, or when a big outage drives everyone to one channel.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`contact_type`, `channel`, `u_channel`, `opened_at`).
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `ch` with a lookup for legacy values; (2) Exclude automated monitoring tickets via `caller_id` or `category` filters; (3) Review rising phone share weekly with service owners when portal adoption programs are active.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-180d@d
| eval ch=lower(coalesce(contact_type, channel, u_channel, "unknown"))
| eval _time=coalesce(opened_at, _time)
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count by _time, ch
| eventstats sum(count) as week_total by _time
| eval share_pct=round(100*count/nullif(week_total,0),1)
| sort _time ch
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Channel Mix Trending** ŌĆö Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service ROI; trending aligns workforce management with ITIL service desk strategy and digital adoption goals.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`contact_type`, `channel`, `u_channel`, `opened_at`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **ch** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **_time** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, ch** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
ŌĆó `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **share_pct** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow list or form that matches this data (for example the incident, request, or problem view). Reconcile a few record numbers, states, and times. Confirm the add-on poll or export time lines up with what you see in the browser.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked area chart (channel share % over weeks), Line chart (portal % trend), Table (raw counts by channel).
grandmaExplanation: We trend how people reach us by phone, portal, and chat, so we staff and train for the way the world really contacts us.
